text.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ، [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْراً مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ])).
text.arabicTranslated: Allaahumma laa tu'aakhithnee bimaa yaqooloona, waghfir lee maa laa ya'lamoona [waj'alnee khayram-mimmaa yadhunnoon]
text.translated: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine].

title: What a Muslim should say when he is praised
